20: impl StageRunner {
21:     pub fn run(&self, task: &mut Task, _api: &CoreHostApi) -> FlowControl {
22:         // Clear stage-scoped flags at the start of each stage (spec requirement)
23:         task.flags.stage.clear();
24: 
25:         let mut i = 0;

27:             code: FLOW_CONTROL_CONTINUE,
28:             payload: std::ptr::null(),
29:         };
30: 
31:         while i < self.plugins.len() {
32:             let plugin = &self.plugins[i];
33:             let task_ptr = task as *mut Task as *mut c_void;
34: 
35:             // Update current plugin index for get_metadata("stage.plugin_index")
36:             task.metadata.insert("current_plugin_index".to_string(), i.to_string());
37: 
38:             // FFI call wrapped in catch_unwind to intercept Rust panics
39:             let (fc, is_panic) = match std::panic::catch_unwind(std::panic::AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
40:                 plugin.plugin.process(plugin.ctx, task_ptr)
41:             })) {
42:                 Ok(fc) => (fc, false),
43:                 Err(_) => (FlowControl { code: FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR, payload: std::ptr::null() }, true),
44:             };
45: 
46:             let error_msg = if is_panic { Some("Plugin panicked".to_string()) } else { None };
47: 
48:             task.history.push(HistoryRecord {
49:                 stage_name: self.name.clone(),
50:                 plugin_name: Some(plugin.name.clone()),
51:                 status: if fc.code == FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR { "Error".to_string() } else { "Completed".to_string() },
52:                 message: error_msg,
53:             });
54: 
55:             if fc.code == FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR {
56:                 // Always call ox_plugin_error — gives the plugin a chance to release its own
57:                 // internal resources (connections, handles) regardless of the on_error policy.
58:                 let task_ptr2 = task as *mut Task as *mut c_void;
59:                 plugin.plugin.error(plugin.ctx, task_ptr2);
60: 
61:                 // Consult on_error policy for StageRunner-handled cases.
62:                 // Stage-name JUMPs and "errored" are returned to FlowRunner.
63:                 match self.on_error_target.as_deref() {
64:                     Some("continue") => {
65:                         // Skip this plugin, continue to the next one in the stage.
66:                         // Task.error_callback is NOT called — task is still running.
67:                         i += 1;
68:                         continue;
69:                     }
70:                     Some("end") => {
71:                         // Terminate the flow cleanly.
72:                         // Task.error_callback is NOT called — not an Errored transition.
73:                         return FlowControl { code: FLOW_CONTROL_END, payload: std::ptr::null() };
74:                     }
75:                     _ => {
76:                         // "errored" (None) or a stage-name: return ERROR to FlowRunner.
77:                         // FlowRunner will handle the stage-name jump or call error_callback

79:                         return fc;
